creator: dom@phmit.demon.co.uk
text:
This version of xscreensaver knows about BSD style shadow passwords,
so locking works correctly.  It still needs to be setuid root, but
it installs itself that way, so it's hopefully not too much of a
problem.  It could always be turned into an optional define if it's
considered to be too big a security risk by default.

I've also commented out the "no-motif" option, in case people have
lesstif installed on there system.  Configure is reasonably sensible
about picking it up.

Fix: Apply this patch:
